Events.EventDescription: Cluster <cluster name> created at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> deleted at <time in UTC>.

We have scheduled a reboot for your cluster <cluster name> for maintenance between <start time in UTC> and <end time in UTC>. Your cluster won't be accessible during this time. We recommend that you plan for the outage to avoid any setbacks.
Amazon Redshift will reboot cluster '<cluster name>' now.
Cluster <cluster name> rebooted at <time in UTC>.
Amazon Redshift rebooted cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> node replaced at <time in UTC>. Cluster operating normally.
Master credentials updated for cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.

The master credentials for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> failed to update at <time> due to concurrent activity. Allow the current workload to complete or reduce the active workload and then retry the operation.
Amazon Redshift can't access the secret for your cluster <cluster name>.
Amazon Redshift can't access the KMS key <kms key> that was used to encrypt the admin credentials secret for your cluster <cluster name>.
Amazon Redshift can't rotate the secret for your cluster <cluster name> because there's an ongoing operation on the cluster.
Your cluster <cluster name> is paused. Amazon Redshift can't rotate the secrets of paused clusters.
The secret for the admin credentials of your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> is currently marked for deletion.

The secret %s for the admin credentials of your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> cannot be deleted. If the secret is being replicated in AWS Secrets Manager, you must first delete all its replicas. You may schedule the secret deletion directly in AWS Secrets Manager.

Amazon Redshift was unable to complete the secret rotation for the admin credentials of your cluster <cluster name>. The admin credentials of your cluster are stored in the version %s of the secret %s in AWS Secrets Manager. If necessary, please move the AWSCURRENT staging label to that version in Secrets Manager.

Parameter group <parameter group name> changes were associated with cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>. If you changed only dynamic parameters, associated clusters are being modified now. If you changed static parameters, all updates, including dynamic parameters, will be applied when you reboot the associated clusters.
VPC security groups for cluster <cluster name> updated at <time in UTC>.
Maintenance started on cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
Maintenance on cluster <cluster name> completed at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> resize started at <time in UTC>. Cluster is in read-only mode.
A resize request for cluster <cluster name> has been acknowledged.
Cluster <cluster name> resize completed at <time in UTC>. Cluster is available for both reads and writes.
Cluster <cluster name> resize failed at <time in UTC>. Operation will be retried within a few minutes.
The requested resize operation for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> was cancelled in the past. Rollback completed at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> elastic resize started at <time in UTC>. Cluster is in read-only mode.
An elastic resize request for cluster <cluster name> has been received.
Cluster <cluster name> elastic resize failed at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> parameter group changed to <parameter group name> at <time in UTC>.
VPC <VPC name> does not exist. Configuration changes for cluster <cluster name> were not applied.
Subnets in cluster subnet group <subnet name> for Amazon VPC <VPC name> do not exist or are invalid. Cluster <cluster name> could not be created.
Subnets in cluster subnet group <subnet name> have no available IP addresses. Cluster <cluster name> could not be created.
VPC <VPC name> has no Internet gateway. Configuration changes for cluster <cluster name> were not applied.
Cluster restore <cluster name> from snapshot <snapshot name> started at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> was restored from cluster snapshot <snapshot name> at <time in UTC>.
Cluster restore <cluster name> from cluster snapshot <snapshot name> failed at <time in UTC>. Retry from the Amazon Redshift console.
A VPC security group is invalid. Configuration changes for cluster <cluster name> were not applied.
An EC2 security group <security group name> specified in cluster security group <security group name> was not found. Authorization cannot be completed.
Security group of cluster <cluster name> modified at <time in UTC>. Changes will be automatically applied to all associated clusters.
Cluster <cluster name> has invalid AWS KMS key permissions. Your cluster will enter an "INACCESSIBLE_KMS_KEY" state and you will no longer be able to access it.
Cluster <cluster name> has now been restored to its previous state because AWS KMS key permissions have been restored.
Unable to connect to HSM for cluster <cluster name>.  Please consult the documentation for troubleshooting details.
Unable to register HSM for cluster <cluster name>. Please provide a different configuration.

Amazon Redshift exceeded your account's EC2 Network Interface (ENI) limit. Please delete up to <maximum number of ENIs> ENIs or request a limit increase of the number of network interfaces per region with EC2.

Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> cannot be created since your account's VPC Endpoint limit has been exceeded. Please delete unused VPC Endpoints or request a limit increase of the number of VPC Endpoint per VPC with EC2.

We have detected that the attempt to load sample data on your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> didn't succeed. Please configure your VPC in order to have access to S3 buckets. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/mgmt/enhanced-vpc-enabling-cluster.html. If you want to have sample data loaded into your cluster, please configure VPC first then create a new cluster.
Your Snapshot <snapshot name> was successfully copied from region <source region> to <destination region> at <time in UTC>.
S3 bucket <S3 bucket name> is no longer valid for logging for cluster <cluster name>.
The S3 Bucket <S3 bucket name> does not have the necessary IAM policies for cluster <cluster name>.
S3 bucket <S3 bucket name> does not exist. Logging cannot be continued for cluster <cluster name>.
Cluster <cluster name> cannot be created.  EIP <ip address> is currently in use.
Cluster <cluster name> cannot be created. EIP <ip address> could not be found.
Cross Region Snapshot Copy is not enabled for cluster <cluster name>.
Cluster renamed to <new cluster identifier> at <time in UTC>.
A table restore request for cluster <cluster name> has been received.
Table restore failed to start for cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>. Reason: <reason>
Table restore started for cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
Table restore completed successfully for cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
Table restore failed for cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
Table restore was cancelled for cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
Replacement for cluster <cluster name> was started at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being automatically restored from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being automatically restored from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.
Cluster <cluster name> has failed with no backups. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status and can be deleted.
Cluster <cluster name> began restart at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> completed restart at <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> node replacement started at <time in UTC>.
Connectivity issue detected on cluster <cluster name>.
Automated recovery action failed on cluster <cluster name>.
Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being automatically restored from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being automatically restored from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed due to a hardware issue. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.

Cluster <cluster name> cannot be replaced because the KMS key <kms key> is inaccessible. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.

Cluster <cluster name> cannot be replaced because the grant limit was reached for the KMS key <kms key>. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Revoke unnecessary grants for the key in AWS KMS and restore the cluster from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.

Cluster <cluster name> cannot be replaced because Amazon Redshift could not create the required grants using the KMS key <kms key>. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.

We identified a hardware maintenance event that affects your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name>. We scheduled a cluster replacement that will start during the next maintenance window. To avoid an unexpected outage, check your current maintenance window and move the window, if needed, to a time before <time in UTC>.

We identified a hardware maintenance event that affects a node in your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name>. We scheduled a node replacement that will start during the next maintenance window. To avoid an unexpected outage, check your current maintenance window and move the window, if needed, to a time before <time in UTC>.
Cluster <cluster name> resize was cancelled at <time in UTC>. The operation was cancelled because <reason>. <action needed>.
Customer initiated maintenance started on your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>. The cluster may not be available during maintenance.
Customer initiated maintenance completed on your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
Customer initiated maintenance failed on your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>. Returning the cluster back to its original state.
Your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name>'s track has been modified from <from track> to <to track>.
Cluster <cluster name> elastic resize completed at <time in UTC>. Cluster is available for both read and write operations.
Cluster <cluster name> elastic resize failed at <time in UTC> and is unavailable for both read and write operations. Restore the cluster from the latest snapshot <snapshot name>
Cluster <cluster name> elastic resize failed at <time in UTC> and rollback succeeded on the cluster. Cluster is available for both read and write operations.
Cluster <cluster name> data transfer completed at <time in UTC>.
Resize canceled for cluster <cluster name>. No IP addresses are available for subnets in cluster subnet group <subnet group name>

The <operation> of Redshift cluster <cluster name> did not succeed while acquiring capacity from our capacity pool. We are working to acquire capacity, but for now we have cancelled your request. Delete this cluster and retry later.

The <operation> of Redshift cluster <cluster name> did not succeed while acquiring capacity from our capacity pool. We are working to acquire capacity but for now, we have cancelled your request. Capacity is available in: <alternative availability zones>. Delete this cluster and retry in an alternative availability zone.

We have detected hardware failure on your single node Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name>, which may have resulted in failed queries or intermittent availability of the cluster. Replacing the cluster did not succeed while acquiring capacity from our capacity pool. You will need to restore to a new cluster from a snapshot. Delete this cluster, select the latest available snapshot, and restore a new cluster from that snapshot. This will automatically provision you on healthy hardware.

We have detected hardware failure on your single node Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name>, which may have resulted in failed queries or intermittent availability of the cluster. Replacing the cluster did not succeed while acquiring capacity from our capacity pool. Capacity is available in availability zone: <alternative availability zones>. Delete this cluster, select the latest available snapshot, and restore a new cluster from that snapshot. This will automatically provision your cluster on healthy hardware.
A cancel resize request for cluster <cluster name> has been received.
The cluster snapshot schedule <snapshot schedule> for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster identifier> modified successfully.
The cluster snapshot schedule <snapshot schedule> for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster identifier> did not update successfully. Try again.
Your cluster <cluster name> will be updated for database maintenance between <start time in UTC> and <end time in UTC>. Your cluster won't be accessible. We recommend that you plan for the outage.
Your cluster <cluster name> will be updated for system maintenance between <start time in UTC> and <end time in UTC>. Your cluster won't be accessible. We recommend that you plan for the outage.

One or more nodes on your cluster <cluster name> will be replaced between <start time in UTC> and <end time in UTC>. You can't defer this required maintenance. We recommend that you plan for the outage.

One or more nodes on your cluster <cluster name> is scheduled to be replaced for maintenance between <start time in UTC> and <end time in UTC>. Your cluster won't be accessible. We recommend that you plan for the outage.
The restore operation to create a new Amazon Redshift cluster '%s' from cluster snapshot '%s' failed due to internal error.  Please restore the snapshot into a cluster of different configuration.
The default database name for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> was changed to <database name> at <time in UTC>.

The default database update for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> didn't succeed because database <database name> doesn't exist. Retry the operation, with CreateDatabase set to true. Alternatively, create the database <database name> first, and then retry.
The limit of <database limit> databases for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> has been reached. The create of database <database name> failed.
A request to update database <database name> for cluster <cluster name> has been received.
The cluster <cluster name> pause operation started at <UTC time>.
The cluster <cluster name> was paused at <UTC time>.
The cluster <cluster name> pause operation failed at <UTC time>. Retry it from the Amazon Redshift console.
The cluster <cluster name> resume operation started at <UTC time>.
The cluster <cluster name> was resumed at <UTC time>.
The cluster <cluster name> resume operation failed at <UTC time>. Retry it from the Amazon Redshift console.
The cluster <cluster name> can't be resumed with its previous Elastic IP address <IP address>. We will request a new Elastic IP address and associate it with the cluster.
The cluster <cluster name> can't be resumed with its previous elastic network interface <ENI id>. We will allocate a new elastic network interface and associate it with the cluster node.
An internal failover request for cluster <cluster name> has been initiated
Amazon Redshift successfully relocated your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> from <availability-zone> to <availability-zone> for recovery.
Amazon Redshift <cluster name> received your relocation request. When Availability Zone relocation completes, Amazon Redshift sends an event notification.
Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> was successfully relocated from <availability-zone> to <availability-zone>. You can use the cluster now.
Amazon Redshift has temporarily disabled cluster relocation for your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> due to configuration changes. Try cluster relocation again later.
Relocation the cluster <cluster name> failed and rollback complete. Please try cluster relocation again later.
Data sharing feature for your cluster <cluster name> will be unavailable until the cluster is resumed.
Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> is being created for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>.
Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> is created for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>.
Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>.
Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. The limit for VPC endpoints was exceeded.
Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. The specified subnet isn't valid.
Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. Not enough IP addresses were available in the specified subnet.
Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. The specified security group isn't valid.
Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. The specified VPC isn't valid.
Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. No valid subnets were found.
Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> is being modified for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>.
Modifying Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> completed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>.
Modifying Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. The specified security group wasn't found.
Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> is being deleted for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>.
Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> is deleted for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>.
AWS account ID <account id> authorized account <account id> to create endpoints for Redshift cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
AWS account ID <account id> revoked authorization for account <account id> to access Redshift cluster <cluster name> at <time in UTC>.
The AQUA configuration status changed to <aqua configuration status> on Redshift cluster <cluster name>. This change will take effect after the next cluster reboot.
EC2-Classic to EC2-VPC migration failed for Redshift cluster <cluster id>.
EC2-Classic to EC2-VPC migration succeeded for Redshift cluster <cluster id>.
The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the EC2-Classic cluster and restore to a EC2-VPC cluster from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>.
Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> cannot be created because you exceeded your account's limit of Elastic IP addresses. Delete unused Elastic IP addresses or request a limit increase with EC2.

Amazon Redshift could not create cluster <cluster name> because the necessary EC2 Network Interfaces (ENIs) are missing or inaccessible. Redshift will automatically retry creating the cluster. If the issue persists, you can investigate the CloudTrail logs with the AWS CloudTrail console.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed to be restarted because the KMS key <kms key> is inaccessible. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.
Cluster <cluster name> has failed to be created because the KMS key <kms key> is inaccessible. Please check the key and either re-enable or grant access to it, then try again
Cluster <cluster name> has failed to be restored because the KMS key <kms key> is inaccessible. Please check the key and either re-enable or grant access to it, then try again

Cluster <cluster name> has failed to be renamed because the KMS key <kms key> is inaccessible. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please check the key and either re-enable or grant access to it, then delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.

Cluster <cluster name> has failed to be resized because the KMS key <kms key> is inaccessible. The cluster is being placed in hardware failure status. Please check the key and either re-enable or grant access to it, then delete the cluster and restore from the latest snapshot <snapshot name> created at <time in UTC>. Any database changes made after this time will need to be resubmitted.
Amazon Redshift Multi-AZ cluster <cluster name> has detected a failure at <time in UTC> and triggered an automatic recovery.

Amazon Redshift Multi-AZ cluster <cluster name> successfully recovered at <time in UTC> and is available for use in <first availability-zone>. Secondary compute in another AZ will be available shortly.
Amazon Redshift Multi-AZ cluster <cluster name> has failed to recover at <time in UTC>.

Amazon Redshift Multi-AZ cluster <cluster name> successfully recovered at <time in UTC> and is available for use with compute resources from both <first availability-zone> and <second availability-zone>.

Maintenance on Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> completed at <time in UTC> and is available for use with compute resources in <first availability-zone>. Secondary compute in another AZ will be available shortly.
Resize on Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> completed at <time in UTC> and is available for use in <first availability-zone>. Secondary compute in another AZ will be available shortly.

Amazon Redshift Multi-AZ cluster <cluster name> has detected a failure in <second availability-zone> at <time in UTC> and triggered an automatic recovery. Your Multi-AZ cluster remains online as the primary compute is still available.
The operation to enable Multi-AZ for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> has started at <time in UTC>.
The operation to enable Multi-AZ for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> has successfully completed at <time in UTC>.
The operation to enable Multi-AZ for Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> has failed at <time in UTC>.
The operation to disable Multi-AZ for your Amazon Redshift Multi-AZ cluster <cluster name> has started at <time in UTC>.
The operation to disable Multi-AZ for your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> has successfully completed at <time in UTC>.
The operation to disable Multi-AZ for your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> has failed at <time in UTC>.
The port of Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> modified successfully.

Amazon Redshift couldn’t create cluster <cluster name> because the Service Linked Role (SLR) necessary for this operation is inaccessible. Try creating it again from the Amazon Redshift console. Amazon Redshift will create the SLR automatically.
Your Redshift cluster nodes aren't the RA3 node type. To enable features like disaster recovery, use the RA3 node type for cluster <cluster name>.

Your Redshift cluster is using a port that isn't available for disaster recovery. To enable features like disaster recovery, use a port from the range (8191-8215) or (5431-5455) for cluster <cluster name>.
Your Redshift cluster can't have patches applied. To enable features like disaster recovery, enable cluster patching for cluster <cluster name>.
Your Amazon S3 bucket <S3 bucket name> has been encrypted with an unknown or inaccessible KMS key. Modify the encryption of your Amazon S3 bucket.

The restore operation on the cluster <cluster name> failed because it doesn't have enough disk space available. The operation is being rolled back. Try restoring to a cluster with a different configuration.

The resize operation on the cluster <cluster name> failed because it doesn't have enough disk space available. The operation is being rolled back. Try resizing to a cluster with a different configuration.
An elastic resize request for cluster <cluster name> has been completed.
The encryption key rotation for your Amazon Redshift cluster <cluster name> was not able to complete.
The operation to register your Amazon Redshift namespace <cluster name> to Glue Data Catalog account <account id> was started at <time in UTC>.
The operation to register your Amazon Redshift namespace <cluster name> to Glue Data Catalog account <account id> has successfully completed at <time in UTC>.
The operation to register your Amazon Redshift namespace <cluster name> to Glue Data Catalog account <account id> has failed at <time in UTC>.
The operation to deregister your Amazon Redshift namespace <cluster name> from Glue Data Catalog account <account id> was started at <time in UTC>.
The operation to deregister your Amazon Redshift namespace <cluster name> from Glue Data Catalog account <account id> has successfully completed at <time in UTC>.
The operation to deregister your Amazon Redshift namespace <cluster name> from Glue Data Catalog account <account id> has failed at <time in UTC>.
Amazon Redshift doesn't support DC2 cluster creation when VPC encryption controls are in enforce mode. To use Amazon Redshift, utilize a supported RA3 node type.
Amazon Redshift doesn't support cluster creation when VPC encryption controls are in the enforce-in-progress state. To use Amazon Redshift, wait until your VPC enters monitor or enforce mode.
Amazon Redshift doesn't support VPC encryption controls in enforce mode when require_ssl is set to false on cluster <cluster name>. Retry with require_ssl set to true.

Amazon Redshift doesn't support Disaster Recovery or Multi-AZ for cluster <cluster name> when VPC encryption controls are in enforce mode. To use Disaster Recovery or Multi-AZ, delete your VPC Encryption Controls or move them to monitor mode.

Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. Creation isn't supported when VPC Encryption Controls are in the enforce-in-progress state.

Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. The cluster is not vpc-encryption capable and the specified VPC has Encryption Controls in enforce mode.
Amazon Redshift doesn't support VPC encryption controls in enforce mode. To use Amazon Redshift, turn off enforce mode in your VPC settings.
Creating the Redshift-managed VPC endpoint <endpoint name> failed for Redshift cluster <cluster name> from AWS account ID <account id>. VPC encryption controls aren't supported.
Cluster security group <security group name> created at <time in UTC>.
Cluster security group <security group name> deleted at <time in UTC>.
Cluster security group <security group name> updated at <time in UTC>. Changes will be automatically applied to associated clusters.
Parameter <parameter name> in Parameter Group <parameter group name> was updated to <new value> at <time in UTC>.
Cluster parameter group <parameter group name> created at <time in UTC>.
Cluster parameter group <parameter group name> deleted at <time in UTC>.

Cluster parameter group <parameter group name> was updated at <time in UTC>. If you changed only dynamic parameters, associated clusters are being modified now. If you changed static parameters, all updates, including dynamic parameters, will be applied when you reboot the associated clusters.

Cluster parameter group <parameter group name> was updated at <time in UTC>. If you changed only dynamic parameters, associated clusters are being modified now. If you changed static parameters, all updates, including dynamic parameters, will be applied when you reboot the associated clusters. If this DB parameter group is attached to any DC2 node type cluster, enabling concurrency scaling through a DB parameter group modification will not take effect. Concurrency scaling is not supported on DC2 node types in this region.
Cluster snapshot <snapshot name> for cluster <cluster name> started at <time in UTC>.
Cluster snapshot <snapshot name> for cluster <cluster name> completed successfully at <time in UTC>.
Cluster snapshot <snapshot name> for cluster <cluster name> failed at time <time in UTC>.
Cluster snapshot <snapshot name>' for cluster <cluster name> cancelled at <time in UTC>.
Cluster snapshot <snapshot name> for cluster <cluster name> was deleted at <time in UTC>.

Final cluster snapshot <snapshot name> for cluster <cluster name> succeeded at <time in UTC>.
Final cluster snapshot <snapshot name> for cluster <cluster name> failed at <time in UTC>.
Final cluster snapshot <snapshot name> for cluster <cluster name> cancelled at <time in UTC>.
The scheduled action '<scheduled action name>' was created at <time in UTC>. The first invocation is scheduled at <time in UTC>.
The scheduled action '<scheduled action name>' is scheduled at <time in UTC>.
The scheduled action '<scheduled action name>' at <time in UTC> finished with 'SUCCEEDED' status.
The scheduled action '<scheduled action name>' was skipped at <time in UTC> due to delay.

The scheduled action '<scheduled action name>' was deleted at <time in UTC>.

Reserved-node exchange failed to start for Redshift reserved node <reserved node id> at <time in UTC>.
